# Strategy Study — 24 Algorithms, Two Universes, Walk-Forward Validated

*Generated by the strategy tournament (June 2026). Reproducible via the scripts and
configs referenced below.*

> **Deflation verdict (read first, supersedes all rankings below).** After running
> ~136 backtests on one 9-year regime, every "edge" was put through a Deflated Sharpe /
> PSR test (`strategy/validation.py`, `scripts/deflate.py`). **Nothing clears the
> DSR>0.95 bar.** Best case (the QQQ collar) reaches DSR 0.43 (harsh empirical null)
> to 0.90 (kind theoretical null) — borderline, not certified; Minimum Track Record
> Length is 32–160 years vs our 9. The apparent winners are statistically
> indistinguishable-to-borderline vs the best of 136 noise trials. **Do not treat any
> ranking here as a validated edge.** What's believable rests on external evidence
> (trend-following, volatility-risk-premium are documented across decades/markets),
> not our backtest numbers. The only real test left is forward paper-trading of a
> simple, literature-backed rule.
>
> **Measurement correction.** Earlier, four harness bugs were found and fixed; earlier
> absolute numbers in this doc were also distorted by them:
> 1. **Phantom sector cap.** `main.py` mapped each name to its own "sector" and
>    `risk.py` applied a 30% per-sector cap → every concentrated position was
>    silently clamped to ≤30% invested (huge cash drag). Fixed: per-sector cap is
>    `null`/disabled until real sector data exists (`config/risk.yaml`,
>    `strategy/risk.py`); caps and stops are now explicit opt-in switches
>    (`enforce_caps`, `use_stops` in `strategies.yaml`).
> 2. **Stale benchmark data.** SPY/AAPL/etc. (original demo symbols) ended
>    2021-03-31 because the ingest skips existing zips; SPY's baseline was computed
>    on truncated data. Fixed by `--force` re-ingest.
>
> **Trustworthy LEAN-consistent baselines (fully invested, monthly, 2017→2026),
> via `scripts/benchmark.py`:** SPY 14.1% CAGR / 0.53 Sharpe / 32.5% DD · QQQ 20.2%
> / 0.68 / 34.5% · TQQQ 38.0% / 0.77 / 81.3%. **QQQ's 0.68 Sharpe is the bar to
> beat.** Strategy numbers below predate the fixes and need re-running under the
> corrected harness before strategy-vs-baseline claims are final.

## Purpose

Develop a broad set of representative trading algorithms, backtest them on a common
universe and shared risk layer, and — critically — **validate** the winners
out-of-sample to separate genuine edge from curve-fitting. This is the design doc
§8 discipline made concrete.

## Method

- **Engine:** QuantConnect LEAN, local backtests on yfinance-sourced daily data
  (~4,357 liquid US symbols ingested via `scripts/ingest_data.py`).
- **Framework:** pluggable strategies (`strategy/base.py`, `strategy/registry.py`,
  `strategy/strategies/`), selected by `config/strategies.yaml: active`. Every
  strategy only decides *which names to hold*; the shared risk layer
  (`strategy/risk.py`: ATR risk-based sizing, per-name/sector/sleeve caps, circuit
  breaker) does the sizing. So the comparison isolates **selection signal** under
  identical risk management.
- **Sweep harness:** `scripts/run_tournament.py` (per-strategy backtest + LEAN HTML
  report + stats collection); `scripts/walk_forward.py` (in-sample vs out-of-sample).
- **Capital/window:** $100k, 2017→2026 daily, same `config/risk.yaml` throughout.

## The 24 algorithms

Trend/momentum: trend_ma, golden_cross, momentum_12_1, roc_momentum, macd,
adx_trend. Breakout: breakout_52w, donchian, volatility_breakout, keltner_breakout.
Mean reversion: rsi_reversion, bollinger_reversion, ma_envelope_dip,
zscore_reversion. Allocation/rotation: dual_momentum, sector_rotation,
low_volatility, risk_parity, relative_strength, vol_adjusted_momentum,
momentum_runner, champion_trend_haven, trend_filtered_hold. Benchmark:
equal_weight_hold.

## Finding 1 — Universe dominates signal

On the original **single-theme AI-semis universe (13 names)**, *no active timing
strategy beat buy-and-hold.* One stock (AXTI, ~20×) dominated; buy-and-hold won
purely by never being shaken out (CAGR 37.7%, but MaxDD 46.9%). Every timing rule
got whipsawed out and missed the recovery.

On a **diversified universe (11 sector ETFs + indices + bonds + gold + intl)**, the
same strategies flipped to positive — they could rotate to safety. Example: MACD
Sharpe −0.76 → +0.50; CAGR −0.7% → +9.7%. **Diversification, not signal cleverness,
was the dominant lever.** Results: `reports/tournament_aisemis.json`,
`reports/tournament_diversified.json`.

## Finding 2 — Simplicity beat complexity

The hand-built hybrid `champion_trend_haven` (MACD trend + bonds/gold safe-haven
rotation) achieved the lowest drawdown in the field (10.1%) but **did not beat plain
MACD** on Sharpe. Added machinery cost return.

## Finding 3 (the important one) — Robustness ≠ peak backtest

Walk-forward split (in-sample 2017–mid'21 vs out-of-sample mid'21–'26), diversified
universe, ranked by **out-of-sample** Sharpe:

| Strategy | IS Sharpe | OOS Sharpe | Decay | OOS MaxDD |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| momentum_12_1 | 0.31 | **0.23** | 0.08 | 8.8% |
| macd | 0.86 | 0.19 | **0.67** | 15.8% |
| dual_momentum | 0.19 | 0.12 | 0.08 | 6.7% |
| momentum_runner | 0.72 | 0.10 | 0.63 | 12.6% |
| donchian | 0.78 | 0.07 | 0.71 | 7.6% |
| champion_trend_haven | 0.76 | −0.03 | 0.78 | 6.7% |
| equal_weight_hold | 0.33 | −0.39 | 0.73 | 22.6% |

**MACD, the tournament leader, was largely overfit** — the biggest Sharpe decay in
the field. The unglamorous **12-1 momentum** and **dual momentum** barely decayed
and held the best out-of-sample risk-adjusted returns. High in-sample Sharpe was a
*warning sign*, not a virtue. Results: `reports/walkforward.json`.

## Finding 4 — Vol-targeting overlay is universe-dependent (and the watchlist is under-deployed)

*Iteration via `/evolve-strategy`, 2026-06-16. Ledger run_id
`2026-06-16T19:00:00+00:00-vol_target_momentum-6023` (scored), plus the
`walk_forward` rows on each universe.*

**Hypothesis (one variable vs `momentum_12_1`):** adding a 200-day trend regime
gate + time-series volatility targeting on top of the same 12-1 momentum signal
lifts OOS Sharpe without added decay (Barroso–Santa-Clara; Faber).

**Result — it depends entirely on the universe:**

| Universe | Candidate OOS Sharpe | Baseline OOS Sharpe | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| diversified (ETF sleeves) | **+0.32** | −0.63 | overlay helps |
| watchlist (12 tech names)  | **−0.75** | −0.59 | overlay **hurts** → reject |

On the broad ETF universe the overlay flipped a losing baseline positive. On the
concentrated 12-name tech book it did the opposite — it loses to the baseline on
the OOS split *and* decays more. Intuition: on a single high-beta sleeve you want
to ride, cutting exposure in high vol just sits out the recovery, and selection is
inert (top_n 20 ≥ 12 names ⇒ holds everything).

**Measurement caveat (flagged, not ignored):** both candidate and baseline are
**under-deployed** on the watchlist — turnover ~0.6–1.7%, net −6% to −12% over a
tech *bull* market. The ATR risk-sizer barely invests on a 12-name book, so the
watchlist walk-forward is measurement-tainted. Fix deployed capital before trusting
any watchlist number.

**Gate: REJECT for the watchlist.** Single-split only, so even the diversified win
is provisional — and per the multiple-testing rule (~100+ trials), an OOS Sharpe
of ~0.3 sits in the noise band and must clear a **deflated Sharpe / PSR** test
before it earns more than a tactical look. Next: (1) fix watchlist deployment,
(2) re-validate the overlay on the *diversified* universe multi-split + DSR.

## Finding 5 — On the watchlist, the OOS bottleneck is selection re-entry, not sizing

*Iteration 2 via `/evolve-strategy`, 2026-06-16. Ledger run_id
`2026-06-16T19:45:00+00:00-momentum_12_1-28d2`.*

Finding 4 left a measurement flag: `momentum_12_1` deployed only **~15% mean
exposure** on the watchlist (confirmed from the backtest Exposure chart). Iteration
2 changed **one variable** — sizing mode `risk_based → vol_target` (the "percentage"
exposure lever) — to test whether sizing was the cause.

| Split | Mean exposure | Sharpe | CAGR |
|---|---|---|---|
| IS 2017–21  | **0.80** | **+0.47** | **+13.5%** |
| OOS 2021–26 | **0.14** | −0.59 | −0.85% |

**vol_target sizing fixed deployment *when names are held*** (IS 15%→80%, Sharpe
+0.47). But **OOS exposure stayed ~14%** and OOS Sharpe (−0.59) matched the
risk_based baseline (−0.59). The bottleneck OOS is **selection, not sizing**: the
positive-12-1-momentum filter empties the book in the 2022 bear and re-enters too
slowly (12-month lookback) to catch the 2023–24 recovery — so there is nothing for
the sizing lever to size. The large decay (1.057) is regime-driven (bull IS, bear→
recovery OOS).

**Gate: REJECT as an OOS improvement** (ties baseline), but a valuable diagnostic:
the exposure lever works, yet on a 12-name book what governs OOS is **re-entry
speed of the selection signal**. Next hypothesis: a faster re-entry rule (shorter
momentum horizon / trend-MA re-entry) so the strategy rejoins the recovery instead
of sitting in cash.

## Finding 6 — Three watchlist rejections: the 12-name universe is the problem, not the signal

*Iteration 3 via `/evolve-strategy`, 2026-06-16. Ledger run_id
`2026-06-16T20:30:00+00:00-roc_momentum-2c5f`.*

Hypothesis: a faster momentum lookback (`roc_momentum`, 6-month) re-enters quicker
after the 2022 crash than 12-1 momentum and lifts OOS. **Rejected** — roc 6mo was
*worse* in both windows (OOS −0.87 vs −0.59; IS −0.18 vs +0.47) and deployed less
(IS exposure 0.53 vs 0.80). A shorter lookback added whipsaw, not better re-entry.

**Meta-pattern across iterations 1–3 (all on the watchlist):**

| Iter | Change vs baseline | OOS result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | vol-target overlay (selection) | reject (−0.75) |
| 2 | vol_target *sizing* | reject OOS (−0.59); fixed IS deployment only |
| 3 | faster lookback (6mo) | reject (−0.87) |

No selection or sizing tweak rescues a momentum strategy on **12 concentrated tech
names** — it under-deploys in/after drawdowns and whipsaws. This re-confirms
**Finding 1 (universe dominates signal)** from the other direction: the lever isn't
another signal tweak, it's the **universe**. Stop optimizing selection/sizing on the
watchlist. Next: take the one thing that *worked* — the vol-target overlay won
out-of-sample on the **diversified** universe — and harden it there (multi-split +
deflated Sharpe), and/or broaden the watchlist toward a real cross-section.

## Finding 7 — The vol-target overlay repeatably beats baseline OOS on diversified (→ tactical)

*Iteration 4 via `/evolve-strategy`, 2026-06-16. Ledger run_id
`2026-06-16T21:15:00+00:00-vol_target_momentum-250a`. First non-rejection.*

Pivoting off the watchlist (Finding 6), tested the vol-target overlay
(`vol_target_momentum`) vs plain `momentum_12_1` on the **diversified** universe,
sizing held at vol_target so the only variable is the overlay.

| Strategy | IS Sharpe | OOS Sharpe |
|---|---|---|
| **vol_target_momentum** | −0.263 | **+0.278** |
| momentum_12_1 | −0.04 | **−1.321** |

The overlay beats the baseline OOS by a wide margin, and the win is **repeatable**:
the earlier legacy diversified run (risk_based sizing) also showed it winning
(+0.32 vs −0.63). Two sizing modes, same direction.

**Gate: TACTICAL, not satellite.** Two honest brakes: (1) OOS +0.278 sits *below*
the ~0.3–0.4 deflated-Sharpe noise band for ~100+ trials — it **fails DSR**, so it
is not yet "good"; (2) it does not beat buy-and-hold (SPY 0.53, QQQ 0.68). It earns
a bounded exploratory (≤10% tactical) look because it reliably beats the strategy
baseline, nothing more. Next: a real **multi-split + formal deflated Sharpe** pass
to see whether it clears the bar and earns satellite — and an honest comparison to
the buy-hold baselines.

## Finding 8 — Multi-split kills the overlay's "edge": it's a crash hedge, not alpha

*Iteration 5 via `/evolve-strategy`, 2026-06-16. Ledger run_id
`2026-06-16T22:00:00+00:00-vol_target_momentum-6382`. Downgrades Finding 7.*

Finding 7 promoted `vol_target_momentum` to tactical on one split. Iteration 5 added
a second split boundary to test robustness:

| Split (OOS window) | overlay OOS | baseline OOS | winner |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2021-06-30 (incl. 2022 crash) | **+0.278** | −1.321 | overlay |
| 2022-06-30 (the recovery)     | +0.359 | **+0.608** | **baseline** |

The overlay **loses to the baseline on the second split**, so it fails the
"beat baseline on ALL splits" gate → **reject for satellite, tactical downgraded.**
The mechanism is now clear: the overlay is a **crash hedge**, not robust alpha — its
regime gate/vol scaling wins when the test window *contains* a drawdown it sidesteps,
and loses when the window is a *recovery* it sits out of. The single-split "+0.278
win" was regime placement, exactly the noise the deflated-Sharpe caution flagged.

**Lesson banked:** the multi-split gate caught regime-luck that a single split would
have shipped. Across iterations 1–5, no momentum/overlay/sizing tweak produced
split-robust alpha on these two universes. The evidence keeps pointing at the
**universe** (Finding 1, 6): a broad cross-section where momentum can actually
diversify, or exiting degraded mode (fundamentals), is the next real lever — not
more signal/sizing variants.

## Finding 9 — Breadth fixes deployment (the universe lever, confirmed) — but not OOS

*Iteration 6 via `/evolve-strategy`, 2026-06-16. Ledger run_id
`2026-06-16T22:45:00+00:00-momentum_12_1-882f`. Tests the Finding 6/8 thesis.*

Built a **200-name** cross-section (`build_universe.py --n 200`, monthly rebalance)
and ran plain `momentum_12_1` — the same signal that under-deployed to ~15% on the
12-name watchlist.

| Window | Exposure | Sharpe | CAGR |
|---|---|---|---|
| IS 2017–21  | **1.07** | **0.75** | **+27.7%** |
| OOS 2021–26 | 0.46 | −0.322 | −3.0% |

**The universe lever is confirmed:** breadth fixed deployment (IS exposure
0.15→1.07) and momentum delivered QQQ-like in-sample numbers (27.7% CAGR, 0.75
Sharpe). This is the structural fix the watchlist could never provide — exactly what
Findings 1/6/8 pointed to.

**But two brakes keep it an OOS reject:** (1) large decay (0.75→−0.32) on the
crash-heavy 2021-06-30 split; (2) **survivorship bias** — the universe is built from
*currently-liquid* names, excluding delisted ones, so the IS is research-grade and
inflated (per the data rule). Breadth is **necessary** (fixes deployment) but **not
sufficient** (OOS regime decay remains). Next: multi-split on the broad universe to
see whether the OOS failure is specific to the 2022-crash split (as in Finding 8),
plus a survivorship-aware read.

## Recommendation

If trading any of this: favor **classic 12-1 / dual momentum on a diversified,
multi-asset universe** — modest but durable (OOS Sharpe ~0.12–0.23, single-digit
drawdowns) — over the eye-catching but fragile MACD. And **never trust a full-period
backtest without a walk-forward split.**

## Caveats (do not skip)

- Daily close data only; no intraday, no realistic transaction-cost/slippage stress.
- Single train/test split — a rolling/anchored walk-forward and parameter-stability
  test would harden these conclusions further.
- Selection compared under one fixed risk-sizing scheme; bespoke weighting untested.
- Two universes only; broader cross-sectional universes (using the 4,357 ingested
  symbols) remain unexplored.
- Still price-only (degraded mode): quality/valuation/sentiment factors not active.

## Reproduce

```bash
export PATH="$HOME/Library/Python/3.9/bin:$PATH"
python3 scripts/ingest_data.py                      # data (once)
python3 scripts/run_tournament.py                   # all strategies, one universe
python3 scripts/walk_forward.py --strategies macd,momentum_12_1,dual_momentum,...
# switch the live strategy:  edit config/strategies.yaml -> active: <name>
lean backtest .
```
